evidence: Since 2019, we have meaningfully expanded our investment in people, systems, and automation to enhance our decision-making, efficiency, and risk management across the full investment lifecycle.
Our predictive analytics platform uses machine learning models trained on proprietary financial and leasing data across more than 15,500 properties, combined with millions of external data points.
These tools are built and supported by dedicated data science, machine learning engineering, and business analysis teams.

A key component of our platform is predictive analytics, which is embedded in our business system and applied to underwriting acquisitions through ongoing portfolio optimization and disposition decisions.
This technology foundation has supported our evaluation of more than $50 billion in transaction volume to date and reinforces our discipline in underwriting and capital allocation.

Our One Team leverages these proprietary capabilities daily, including predictive analytics, a tailored ERP platform with source-to-book workflow automation, and robotic process automation (RPA) initiatives.

The risk of a cybersecurity breach or operational disruption has generally increased as the number, intensity and sophistication of attempted attacks (including through the use of artificial intelligence) have increased.

Our use of or inability to adopt and deliver new technological capabilities, including artificial intelligence and machine learning, may put us at a competitive disadvantage or adversely impact our business, reputation, results of operations.

The use of emerging technologies, including artificial intelligence, entails risks including intellectual property infringement; data privacy; potential for inaccuracy; bias; new or enhanced governmental or regulatory scrutiny; cybersecurity concerns.

limitations: No quantified dollar amounts for AI-specific investment were found in the excerpts; investment intensity scored 3 rather than 4 or 5.
The $50 billion figure references transaction volume evaluated using the platform, not AI investment spend itself.
Data center joint venture capex ($216.8 million remaining estimated costs) relates to real estate development with Digital Realty, not directly to Realty Income's own AI investment.

Risk disclosure covers named categories and mechanisms (competitive disadvantage, regulatory scrutiny, cybersecurity) but no quantified risk exposure or named governance body was found, capping score at 4.
